As a highly dedicated student that is well- accomplished in the fields of customer service and communication capabilities and proficient to the world of technology and administration, I strongly believe that I will have a positive impact towards your company. This is shown though my 4 years of experience as a Retail Associate at McDonalds, 10+ months of experiences as an Administrative Assistant and Support, and through my previous extracurricular experiences. I've been thinking about my fueling strategy, and I plan to use a combination of energy gels, sports drinks, and water to stay fueled and hydrated during the marathon. I've been practicing with different types of fuel during my long runs to see what works best for me.

I'm also planning to take advantage of the aid stations along the course, especially for water and electrolyte replenishment. I've heard that it's crucial to stay on top of hydration and electrolyte levels during a long race like a marathon.

One thing I'm still deciding on is whether to use a hydration belt or rely on the aid stations for water. I've heard pros and cons of both approaches, and I'm not sure what will work best for me.

Do you have any advice on hydration belts vs. aid stations? Have you heard of any specific products or brands that work well for runners?

from the book "Golden Grant Rules" by David Kincade.
IMPROVE YOUR WRITING
75. Advice from the grant judge's mouth
Almost every grant agency staff person you speak to wants to read a clear and compelling application. One staffer explained to me how this works at the judging stage.
"What usually happens is we'll read a dozen or so, and a clearly-written application will make it to the "next" pile (for further consideration). Now having said that, the technology needs to be great as well. But you have to understand something. We are all busy volunteers. We want the application easy to understand."
Grant Tip
Impressive technology and clear writing create a winnable grant application. First, speak to the grant agency to confirm they love your innovation. If so, make sure the writing is good!
76. Write shorter sentences
You do not need to be a professional writer to win grants, but you need the basics. The most common mistake I see in writing are sentences that span two or three lines. Stop!
You can have longer type sentences, but you should use short sentences as well. Give the reader a rest. A longer sentence followed by a shorter one helps the reader. Here is another place you can improve your writing: http://www.plainenglish.co.uk/free- guides.html
Grant Story
I've known grant agencies to help people they know who are terrible at writing with their applications.
58
77. Use active voice
Learning to write in the active voice will help all your writing, not just your grants.
According to Purdue University's excellent writing centre, "Active voice is used for  …[truncated]

This is the first service page. I only want you to reply "acknowledged" to remember the first content. I will give you the next content after you acknowledge the first service page. 
Contract disputes
Contracts are legally binding agreements between two or more parties who promise to do (or not do) something in return for payment of some sort.
What is a Contract?
Contrary to popular belief, a contract does not need to be in writing — it can be oral, part oral and part written, or even implied by conduct.

Entering a contract is a regular part of life, whether you are aware of it or not. For instance, you enter into a contract when you buy a loaf of bread, negotiate a pay rise or buy a movie ticket.

Most contract disputes arise when a party to the contract breaches a term that is written or implied in the contract.

A breach of contract usually occurs through:

a failure to perform on time;
a failure to perform to an agreed standard; or
a refusal or inability to perform at all.
A breach of contract can have a severe impact on people and businesses. Whether you are the plaintiff or the defendant in a contract dispute, our team of dedicated contract lawyers in Brisbane can provide expert legal advice to ensure your rights are protected.

Business disputes for an agreement, contract, startup, etcetera.Business
Consumer disputes regarding money, business, assets, etcetera.Consumer

I will give you a transcript from an NJ PBS segment. Can you summarize it in 200 words and print a list of three interesting quotes from the transcript?

The Paterson Police Department is once again under scrutiny, this time after a 31 year old man who works with the city's Violence Intervention Program was fatally shot by officers over the weekend. The incident followed an hours-long standoff while the man, Najee Seabrooks, was barricaded inside his apartment reportedly experiencing a mental health crisis. Local activists are circulating a petition with more than 4,300 signatures today condemning the police department and demanding a total restructuring as those who worked closely with Seabrooks to prevent shootings like this say they were blocked from providing the help they were trained to give. Senior political correspondent David Cruz reports.
Patterson Mayor Andre Sayegh wasn't saying much today. Three days after a four-hour standoff ended with police shooting violence interventionist, Najee Seabrooks. The mayor didn't answer any questions about the shooting when he appeared at an unrelated event this morning,
Over four hours where the police were trying to de-escalate the situation. They actually enlisted the assistance of one of his family members, unfortunately was unsuccessful. We are mourning the loss. I've spoken to his mother as well, but we want to make sure that this investigation plays out.
The family member the mayor refers to is a police officer from another jurisdiction. Members of the St. Joseph Hospital based Paterson Healing Collect …[truncated]
